title: Rotated Sorted Array
problem:
Suppose an array of length n sorted in ascending order is rotated between 1 and n times. For example,
the array nums = (0,1,2,4,5,6,7) might become:
• 14,5,6,7,0,1,2) if it was rotated 4 times.
• 10,1,2,4,5,6, 7) if it was rotated 7 times.
Notice that rotating an array (a(01, a(1], a(2], ..., a[n-1ll 1 time results in the array (a(n-1], a(01, a(ll, a(21, ..., a(n-2]].
Given the sorted rotated array nums of unique elements, return the minimum element of this array.
You must write an algorithm that runs in O(log n) time.
Input: nums = [3,4,5,1,2]
There is an integer array nums sorted in ascending order (with distinct values).
Prior to being passed to your function, nums is possibly rotated at an unknown pivot index k (1 <= k < nums. length) such that the resulting array is (nums(k), nums(k+1), ..., nums(n-1), nums(0), nums (1], ..., nums(k-1]l (0-indexed). For example, 10,1,2,4,5,6,71 might be rotated at pivot index
3 and become (4,5,6,7,0,1,2).
Given the array nums after the possible rotation and an integer target, return the index of target if it is in nums, or -1 if it is not in nums .
You must write an algorithm with 0(log n) runtime complexity.
Input: nums = [4,5,6,7,0,1,2], target = 0
